resume:
Devin Park — Junior Supply Chain Analyst

Profile
Early-career analyst focused on procurement operations and inbound logistics, with a habit of framing work in problem / action / result terms.

Experience
Harborline Goods — Procurement Analyst (2023-present)
- Situation: Inbound POs were being acknowledged late, with supplier acknowledgment SLA sitting around 60%.
- Task: I was asked to lift on-time PO acknowledgment to 85% within one quarter.
- Action: I built a daily exception report in Excel flagging un-acknowledged POs past 24h, set up a standing follow-up cadence with three key suppliers, and proposed an auto-reminder email rule.
- Result: Acknowledgment SLA rose from 60% to 88% in eleven weeks; expedite freight on those lines dropped noticeably.

Crestfield Retail — Supply Chain Intern (Summer 2022)
- Reconciled a 400-line SKU master and cleared 70+ duplicate part numbers.

Skills: procurement ops, Excel, PO management, supplier follow-up, data cleanup.
Education: BS, Operations & Supply Chain Management.
jd: Harborline Goods is hiring a Junior Supply Chain Analyst to support procurement operations under the Procurement Manager. You will own the daily PO exception process, chase supplier acknowledgments, maintain item-master data hygiene, and help reduce expedite freight on inbound lines. We are looking for someone who can describe their own work concretely: what the problem was, what they personally did about it, and what changed as a result, with real numbers. You will inherit a manual acknowledgment-tracking process and a supplier base with inconsistent responsiveness. Comfort in Excel is required; exposure to an ERP (SAP, NetSuite, or similar) is a plus. This is an early-career role with strong mentorship and a clear path into planning or category management.

history.q: Your resume says you lifted supplier PO acknowledgment from 60% to 88%. Walk me through what you specifically did — the actual steps, not the summary.

candidate_last_answer: Yeah, so it was really just about staying on top of things and being proactive. I kept an eye on the POs, made sure suppliers were responsive, and just generally drove accountability. It was a team effort to be honest, everyone pitched in, and over time things just got a lot better. I think the key was good communication and staying organized.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: staying on top of things and being proactive

expected-yield: forces the candidate to name the concrete mechanism behind the 60-to-88% lift — the exception report, the follow-up cadence with which suppliers, and which step they personally built versus the team — instead of generic 'proactive' and 'communication' filler
